summary: Composio multi-channel integration GREEN end-to-end. Backend pytest 17/17 passing: status (founder + premium plan_allows=true, api_key_configured=false), connect/messenger 503 with 'COMPOSIO_API_KEY no configurada', whatsapp-provider toggle composio↔cloud_api persists + 422 on invalid value, webhook (permissive dev mode) creates contacts for instagram/messenger/whatsapp via Composio with fuente=composio_*, dedup by sender_id confirmed (instagram_id second-message → no duplicate), send returns 409 'no conectado', disconnect idempotent 200, regression on /api/crm/{contacts,metrics,pipeline,settings,ai/logs,ai/priorities} all 200. Frontend: ComposioSection renders 3 cards in NO CONECTADO state, composio-no-api-key banner visible, provider toggle works (Composio→reload→still active→Cloud API revert→active), Conectar Messenger triggers native alert with API key error (captured via dialog). Hoy section: 0 console errors after navigation.

minor.issue: Contact pydantic model in /app/backend/crm.py does NOT declare instagram_id / messenger_id, so these fields are stripped from API responses (extra='ignore'). Mongo persists them correctly via upsert_contact_from_signal, but UI/integrations cannot read them via /api/crm/contacts. Add Optional[str] instagram_id / messenger_id to the Contact model.

When an existing contact is matched by phone, fuente is never updated. A pre-existing whatsapp contact stays fuente='whatsapp' even when a Composio inbound arrives — could complicate analytics by provider. Consider tracking provider history (e.g. last_provider) or accept that fuente reflects first touch only (current behavior).

action_items: Add `instagram_id: Optional[str] = None` and `messenger_id: Optional[str] = None` to Contact model in /app/backend/crm.py so frontend can show channel chips/identifiers without extra DB queries.
(Optional) Decide policy on `fuente` for already-existing contacts touched by a different provider — current upsert keeps the original fuente.
critical_code_review_comments: composio_channels.py: clean module, good fallback v3→v1 for connect/send. Webhook in dev (no secret) logs a warning and bypasses HMAC — acceptable for testing, MUST be revisited before going live (set COMPOSIO_WEBHOOK_SECRET).
Channel normalization (_normalize_channel) cleanly maps public 'whatsapp' ↔ internal 'whatsapp_composio'.

ComposioSection.jsx is well structured (~320 LOC), clear sub-components; uses native window.alert/confirm — fine, but could be migrated to shadcn AlertDialog for UI consistency (same comment as iter_10).
Plan gating verified: founder_full and premium both pass; tested via /api/composio/status returns plan_allows=true.
_extract_inbound: parses 3 trigger slugs and supports legacy shapes — solid defensive parsing.
MINOR (already noted): Contact pydantic model is the bottleneck preventing instagram_id/messenger_id from appearing in REST responses.

success_rate.frontend: 100% on verified flows (cards, banner, toggle, persistence, alert capture, regression)

test_credentials: founder@digiactiva.com / digiactiva2025 (token returned at JSON key 'token' — note: NOT 'access_token'); admin@pastaalvuelo.com / pastaalvuelo2025.
seed_data_creation: Tests created 3 composio contacts in DigiActiva workspace: insta_test_42 (composio_instagram), msg_xy (composio_messenger), +56988888042 (composio_whatsapp). Pasta al Vuelo whatsapp_provider was toggled to 'composio' then RESET BACK to 'cloud_api' (verified via /api/composio/status).
context_for_next_testing_agent: All Composio endpoints under /api/composio/* are LIVE and correctly plan-gated (premium/elite/founder_full). data-testids in use: composio-section, composio-card-{messenger|instagram|whatsapp}, composio-connect-{ch}, composio-disconnect-{ch}, composio-status-{not_connected|pending|connected|error}, composio-no-api-key, whatsapp-provider-toggle, provider-cloud-api, provider-composio, composio-refresh. Login auth returns JSON with key 'token' (not access_token). Webhook in dev mode is permissive (no HMAC) — production must set COMPOSIO_WEBHOOK_SECRET. /api/crm/contacts response strips instagram_id / messenger_id (Contact model gap, not a real DB issue). To repro any flow, reuse /app/backend/tests/test_composio.py.
rca of the issue: No bugs blocking. Two minor observations (Contact model missing fields, fuente immutability on upsert) are documented for the main agent.
